# Software Setup {#sec-appendix-software}

This appendix provides detailed instructions for setting up the software environment required to run and develop *shinyplanr* applications.

## R and RStudio

### Installing R

Download and install R from CRAN:

1. Visit [https://cran.r-project.org/](https://cran.r-project.org/)
2. Select your operating system (Linux, macOS, or Windows)
3. Download the latest version (R 4.3.0 or later recommended)
4. Run the installer and follow the prompts

::: {.callout-note}
## R Version
*shinyplanr* requires R version 4.1.0 or later. We recommend using the latest stable release for best compatibility.
:::

### Installing RStudio

RStudio provides an integrated development environment for R:

1. Visit [https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)
2. Download the free RStudio Desktop version for your operating system
3. Run the installer

[IMAGE: RStudio download page showing operating system options]

## Required R Packages

### Core Packages

Install the core packages required for *shinyplanr*:

```r
# CRAN packages
install.packages(c(
  "shiny",
  "golem",
  "shinyjs",
  "shinycssloaders",
  "shinyWidgets",
  "bslib",
  "sf",
  "terra",
  "dplyr",
  "tidyr",
  "ggplot2",
  "leaflet",
  "DT",
  "htmltools"
))
```

### prioritizr

Install *prioritizr* from CRAN or the development version:

```r
# From CRAN (stable)
install.packages("prioritizr")

# Development version (latest features)
install.packages(
  "prioritizr",
  repos = c(
    "https://prioritizr.r-universe.dev",
    "https://cloud.r-project.org"
  )
)
```

### spatialplanr

Install *spatialplanr* from GitHub:

```r
if (!require(remotes)) install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("SpatialPlanning/spatialplanr")
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## Minimum Version
*shinyplanr* requires *spatialplanr* version 0.6.9 or later. Check your version with `packageVersion("spatialplanr")`.
:::

### oceandatr

Install *oceandatr* from GitHub:

```r
options(timeout = 9999)  # Extended timeout for large package
remotes::install_github("emlab-ucsb/oceandatr")
```

### spatialgridr

Usually installed automatically as a dependency, but can be installed manually:

```r
remotes::install_github("emlab-ucsb/spatialgridr")
```

### Complete Installation Script

Run this script to install all required packages:
  
```r
# Function to install if not present
install_if_missing <- function(pkg, ...) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, ...)
  }
}

# Install CRAN packages
cran_packages <- c(
  "shiny", "golem", "shinyjs", "shinycssloaders", 
  "shinyWidgets", "bslib", "sf", "terra", "dplyr", 
  "tidyr", "ggplot2", "leaflet", "DT", "htmltools",
  "remotes", "rsconnect"
)

for (pkg in cran_packages) {
  install_if_missing(pkg)
}

# Install prioritizr
install_if_missing(
  "prioritizr",
  repos = c(
    "https://prioritizr.r-universe.dev",
    "https://cloud.r-project.org"
  )
)

# Set timeout for large GitHub packages
options(timeout = 9999)

# Install GitHub packages
if (!requireNamespace("spatialplanr", quietly = TRUE) || 
    packageVersion("spatialplanr") < "0.6.9") {
  remotes::install_github("SpatialPlanning/spatialplanr")
}

if (!requireNamespace("oceandatr", quietly = TRUE)) {
  remotes::install_github("emlab-ucsb/oceandatr")
}

message("All packages installed successfully!")
```

## Optimisation Solvers

*prioritizr* requires a mathematical programming solver. Several options are available.

### HiGHS (Recommended for Most Users)

*HiGHS* is a high-performance open-source solver that works well for most problems:

```r
# Install the highs R package
install.packages("highs")

# Verify installation
library(highs)
```

*HiGHS* is automatically detected by *prioritizr* when installed.

### CBC (Alternative Open-Source)

CBC (COIN-OR Branch and Cut) is another open-source option:

```r
# Install Rcbc
install.packages("Rcbc", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
```

### Gurobi (Best Performance)

*Gurobi* provides the best performance for large problems but requires a licence. Free academic licences are available.

#### Obtaining Gurobi

1. Visit [https://www.gurobi.com/](https://www.gurobi.com/)
2. Create an account
3. Apply for an academic licence (free for qualifying institutions)
4. Download and install *Gurobi* for your operating system

#### Installing the R Interface

After installing *Gurobi*:

```r
# Find the R package in your Gurobi installation
# (adjust path based on your Gurobi version and OS)

# On macOS:
install.packages("/Library/gurobi1100/macos_universal2/R/gurobi_11.0-0.tar.gz", 
                 type = "source", repos = NULL)

# On Windows:
install.packages("C:/gurobi1100/win64/R/gurobi_11.0-0.tar.gz", 
                 type = "source", repos = NULL)

# On Linux:
install.packages("/opt/gurobi1100/linux64/R/gurobi_11.0-0.tar.gz", 
                 type = "source", repos = NULL)
```

#### Verifying Gurobi

```r
library(gurobi)

# Check licence
gurobi_version()
```

### Solver Comparison

| Solver | Performance | Licence | Best For |
|--------|------------|---------|----------|
| *HiGHS* | Good | Open source | Most users |
| CBC | Moderate | Open source | Basic problems |
| *Gurobi* | Excellent | Commercial (free academic) | Large, complex problems |

### Checking Available Solvers

```r
library(prioritizr)

# List installed solvers
available_solvers <- list(
  HiGHS = requireNamespace("highs", quietly = TRUE),
  CBC = requireNamespace("Rcbc", quietly = TRUE),
  Gurobi = requireNamespace("gurobi", quietly = TRUE)
)

print(available_solvers)
```

## Spatial Libraries

### GDAL, GEOS, and PROJ

The sf and terra packages require system libraries for spatial operations.

#### macOS

Using Homebrew:

```bash
brew install gdal geos proj
```

Or use the pre-compiled binaries that come with the CRAN sf package.

#### Windows

Windows users generally do not need to install these separately - they are bundled with the sf package.

#### Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install libgdal-dev libgeos-dev libproj-dev
```

#### Linux (Fedora/CentOS)

```bash
sudo dnf install gdal-devel geos-devel proj-devel
```

### Verifying Spatial Setup

```r
library(sf)
sf::sf_extSoftVersion()

library(terra)
terra::gdal()
```

## Development Dependencies

For developing *shinyplanr* applications:

### golem

The *golem* package provides the framework for *shinyplanr*:

```r
install.packages("golem")
```

### devtools

For package development:

```r
install.packages("devtools")
```

### usethis

For project setup:

```r
install.packages("usethis")
```

### testthat

For testing:

```r
install.packages("testthat")
```

## Quarto (Optional)

For rendering this manual and other documentation:

1. Visit [https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/)
2. Download and install Quarto for your operating system
3. Restart RStudio after installation

Verify installation:

```bash
quarto --version
```

## Troubleshooting

### Package Installation Fails

If a package fails to install:

1. **Check R version**: Ensure R is up-to-date
2. **Update existing packages**: `update.packages(ask = FALSE)`
3. **Clear package cache**: Restart R and try again
4. **Check system dependencies**: Some packages require system libraries

### sf Installation Issues

If sf fails to install on Linux:

```bash
# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt install libudunits2-dev libsqlite3-dev

# Fedora/CentOS
sudo dnf install udunits2-devel sqlite-devel
```

### GitHub Package Timeout

For large GitHub packages:

```r
options(timeout = 9999)  # Increase timeout
remotes::install_github("package/repo")
```

### Solver Not Found

If *prioritizr* cannot find a solver:

```r
# Ensure solver package is loaded
library(highs)

# Or specify solver explicitly in add_default_solver()
```

### Memory Issues

For large planning problems:

```r
# Increase memory limit (Windows)
memory.limit(size = 16000)

# Use 64-bit R for larger datasets
```

## Verifying Installation

Run this script to verify your installation:

```r
# Check core packages
packages <- c(
  "shiny", "golem", "sf", "terra", "prioritizr", 
  "spatialplanr", "leaflet", "dplyr"
)

check_packages <- function(pkgs) {
  results <- sapply(pkgs, function(pkg) {
    if (requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      paste("OK -", as.character(packageVersion(pkg)))
    } else {
      "MISSING"
    }
  })
  data.frame(Package = names(results), Status = results, row.names = NULL)
}

print(check_packages(packages))

# Check solver
if (requireNamespace("highs", quietly = TRUE)) {
  message("HiGHS solver: Available")
} else if (requireNamespace("gurobi", quietly = TRUE)) {
  message("Gurobi solver: Available")
} else {
  message("WARNING: No solver found")
}

# Check spatial libraries
message("\nSpatial library versions:")
print(sf::sf_extSoftVersion())
```

Expected output should show all packages as "OK" with version numbers, and at least one solver available.

## Summary

To run *shinyplanr*, you need:

1. **R** (version 4.1.0 or later)
2. **RStudio** (recommended)
3. **Core R packages** (*shiny*, *sf*, *terra*, *prioritizr*, *spatialplanr*, etc.)
4. **A solver** (*HiGHS* recommended for most users)
5. **Spatial system libraries** (GDAL, GEOS, PROJ)

Once these are installed, you can proceed to set up your *shinyplanr* application.
